title: Beyond Mean Solar Wind Conditions: Turbulence-Aware Forecasting of the AE Index
summary:
arXiv:2606.16518v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The auroral electrojet (AE) index is a key indicator of high latitude geomagnetic activity and is widely used in operational space weather monitoring, yet forecasting AE from upstream solar wind conditions remains challenging due to nonlinear coupling, internal magnetospheric dynamics, and multiscale variability. We test whether incorporating solar wind turbulence improves short timescale AE forecasts beyond models based only on mean solar wind and interplanetary magnetic field parameters.
